stages.name: release_acceptance
stages.evidence: every docs/PROJECT_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA.md criterion is satisfied from archived deterministic evidence: required_commands (help_text + cli route), required_mutators (8 src/mutators modules implementing the 12 stable operators), required_reports (text/json/jsonl/junit + report.v1 schema), schemas_validate_reports (SCHEMA_REGISTRY subset validator), public_docs_doctest (task 066), final_dogfood_gate (task-085 archived dogfood evidence + release_dogfood_gate.py manifest pass), ci_network_free (scripts/ci.sh, no remote provider), ai_advisory_only (src/ai advisory commands), and ast_stable_default_backends_opt_in (default config backend=ast, experimental=[]). No release blockers were found, so no prerequisite task was inserted and the task is completed rather than blocked.

stages.name: final_dogfood_gate_evidence
stages.evidence: the task-085 final dogfood gate passes: archived deterministic dogfood reports exist and normalize identically, the protected scope has no invalid mutants, and the one protected survivor is recorded with equivalent-risk review evidence.

stages.name: unit_tests
stages.evidence: test/release_acceptance_test verifies the acceptance manifest status agrees with its criteria and blockers (unmet criterion and ignored blocker both rejected), the task-085 dogfood evidence exists, AST is the stable default with experimental backends opt-in, the required commands appear in help_text, the required stable mutators are accessible, and scripts/ci.sh + scripts/release_acceptance.py exercise the release gates; full suite green.

stages.name: mutation_checks
stages.reason: task 060 is release acceptance verification: it adds a checklist script, a Zig acceptance test, acceptance fixtures, and release/roadmap docs, and implements no product behavior (src/** is forbidden). The mutation evidence it verifies is the task-085 final dogfood gate.

residual_risk: Release acceptance is a checklist over archived deterministic evidence; it reuses the task-085 final dogfood gate and the per-commit scripts/ci.sh stages rather than re-running the entire pipeline, so it asserts release readiness from committed artifacts and source/contract presence, not a fresh end-to-end product run.

The one protected-scope dogfood survivor (src/source_map.zig arithmetic) is accepted on deterministic equivalent-risk review evidence rather than a new pinning test; a follow-up unit test of the exact offset arithmetic remains a low-risk hardening item that does not block release.

No release blockers were found, so this task is completed; had a criterion been unmet, the contract requires recording a blocked acceptance manifest with concrete prerequisite task metadata and marking task 060 blocked rather than relaxing the criteria.
